programmes.show_description: Kings And Queens Of 'Cue. Guy Fieri meets some masters of meat and BBQ. A Texas-inspired barbecue joint smokes pork ribs and a Denver eatery tops pizza with beer braised brisket. S17, Ep6
A Festival Of Flavour. Guy Fieri pulls over for the bounty of the county. A Denver bar creates savoury apple strudel and in Arizona a Pakistani spot serves goat and tandoori Chicken. S17, Ep7
Timeless Traditions. Guy Fieri gets the lowdown on some enduring traditions. In Philadelphia he checks out a Jamaican joint's jerk chicken. In Denver, he tries rabbit and chitlings. S17, Ep8
From Pound Cake To Pot Pie. Guy Fieri pulls in for a mishmash of comfort food. He makes stops in Philadelphia, Leucadia, Cali and Phoenix. S17, Ep9
Des Moines, IA. Casey Webb heads to Des Moines, Iowa, where he'll attempt the eat an overstuffed pulled pork sandwich slathered in a scorpion pepper hot sauce in ten minutes. S1, Ep9
Billings, MT. Casey Webb travels to Billings, Montana, to tackle a one-hour steak challenge featuring a 78-ounce rib eye and two pounds of mashed potatoes, salad and bread. S1, Ep10
Mining Cake, Sailing Santa, And Sister Makeovers. After visiting an old zinc mine that glows, the team makes a cake that really rocks. And the sisters get a cosmetic injection! S9, Ep29
A Barter, A Baker, And An Italian Professor. Two tween brothers offer to trade pizza for a pony cake for their sister. Mauro's son has an Italian lesson before a semester in Rome. S9, Ep30

James travels along the stunning Antrim coast and glens of Northern Ireland. Local boy Paul Rankin joins him to brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge. S1, Ep15
Jazz. The bikers return to New Orleans, where Simon tries to convince Dave that jazz can be enjoyable. Plus, they cook classic local dishes, including jambalaya and gumbo. S1, Ep5
Haley's Having A Baby. Ree makes dishes perfect for freezing for a friend who just had a baby. She cooks pasta casserole, white bean and sausage soup and peanut butter cup bites. S22, Ep10
Two Ranchers And Ree. Ree makes lunch for her husband, Ladd, and his dad. She starts with her father-in-law's favourite shrimp brochettes and then the ultimate quesadillas. S22, Ep11
Jamie serves lasagne with roasted duck and shows his take on everybody's favourite, bacon sarnie. Plus, makes some delicious huevos rancheros. S1, Ep7
Jamie prepares the ultimate comfort food with his version of the British classic, fish and chips. And, he gives Indonesian gado gado salad a vegetarian twist. S1, Ep8
Local Food Heroes. Ina, inspired by the fabulous food in the Hamptons, creates new recipes for her blog. She makes salmon and guacamole sandwiches and balsamic roasted beet salad. S8, Ep10
Old New York. Ina learns from New York's food greatest by taking smoked salmon and bagel masterclass at a Jewish store and a steak lesson from a legendary butcher. S9, Ep1
Alternative Ingredients. The bakers must bake without sugar, gluten or dairy - but thankfully not all in the same challenge. Plus, things get sticky in Paul's technical challenge. S6, Ep5
Pastry. It's time for the bakers to prove their pastry skills, and the technical challenge has them all treading carefully as they tackle the delicate world of puff pastry. S6, Ep6
Snowdonia. James heads to North Wales with Stephen Terry. They have a five-star meal at Pale Hall, then James cooks Welsh lamb with gnocchi. S1, Ep7
Pembrokeshire. James and Stephen are heading south through Wales en route to Pembrokeshire. They visit Michelin-starred restaurant Ynyshir, run by chef Gareth Ward. S1, Ep8
Jazz. The bikers return to New Orleans, where Simon tries to convince Dave that jazz can be enjoyable. Plus, they cook classic local dishes, including jambalaya and gumbo. S1, Ep5

James travels along the stunning Antrim coast and glens of Northern Ireland. Local boy Paul Rankin joins him to brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge. S1, Ep15
Northern Ireland - Part Two. James's Northern Ireland journey continues with his mate Paul Rankin. They visit Lough Neagh, the biggest freshwater lake in the British Isles. S1, Ep16
All San Diego, All The Time. Guy Fieri heads to San Diego for some old and new favourites, including a legendary surfer spot for some tasty burgers and a brother-owned noodle joint. S17, Ep11
From The Heart. Guy Fieri visits eateries that put their heart into everything they do. In California, an Italian joint stays true to a family ravioli recipe. S17, Ep12
That's Fresh. In San Francisco, Guy Fieri visits a Venezuelan joint that serves up a totally gluten-free menu. In Portland, OR, a family-run sausage spot makes homemade meats. S17, Ep13
Authentic Eats. Guy Fieri finds real deal authentic eats in unusual spots. In St. Louis, he is being served rice and beans, pot stickers, and pulled pork pasta. S16, Ep1
Boston, MA. Casey Webb travels to historic Boston, Massachusetts, for some epic New England eats and a 4.5-pound Irish breakfast challenge at Flann O'Brien's Pub. S2, Ep1
Burlington, VT. Casey Webb travels to the small town of Burlington, Vermont, for some mammoth eats, including a beer-infused bagel feast and a 3.5-pound breakfast sandwich challenge. S2, Ep2
Everything Old is New Again. Buddy has to convince his momma that after 100 years it is time to give Carlo's a timely facelift, so he cooks up a plan to sweeten the deal. S7, Ep1
Kid Crush and Carnival. Angela, the owner of a Brazilian restaurant, pays a visit to Carlo's to find out if Buddy's team can bake a carnival cake that can match her vibrant parties. S7, Ep2

Sunday Lunch. Michel takes Sunday lunch to the next level. He serves fish quenelles, chicken cooked in salt crust pastry and red wine poached pears with galette serpentine. S1, Ep1
Michel serves up a feast that's made to share. He prepares a smoked fish platter, a rich beef cheek bourguignon and a lattice-topped salted caramel and walnut tart. S1, Ep3
Michel visits a family-run vineyard to get an important ingredient for his monkfish dish. Plus, he reveals his simple recipe for pate grand-mere. S1, Ep2
After fishing in Le Grau-du-Roi, Michel shares his recipe for a flavourful mackerel dish. Later, he cooks a succulent roast chicken with herb butter. S1, Ep3
Richmond. Michel goes fishing with an old friend and makes an alfresco fish dish by the river. Plus, he makes watercress soup, a winter salad, and roast apples. S1, Ep3
Southend-on-Sea. In Southend, Michel meets a family of fishmongers and chefs keeping it local. Plus, he makes scallop curry, prawn toast, and fried custard beignets. S1, Ep5
Windsor. At Royal Windsor, Michel finds the perfect fish and chips and visits Monkey Island. He cooks a mackerel burger, shepherd's pie and a peach meringue. S2, Ep3

programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Classic Combos. Tom creates classic 'double acts' with two key ingredients. On the menu are fish and chips, steak and ale pie and bread and butter pudding. S1, Ep3

programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Spice Route. In the pub, a traditional shepherd's pie is given an Indian twist. Plus, Tom cooks lamb curry, Thai fishcakes and spiced pineapple tarte tatin. S2, Ep8
Fish. Tom brings an all-time favourite dish to his Sunday table: fish and chips. He bakes a beautiful side of salmon in brown butter and makes perfect triple-cooked chips. S1, Ep4
French. Tom pays homage to the South of France. He makes a seafood bouillabaisse with sea bass, red mullet and shellfish, and praline and chocolate profiteroles. S2, Ep8
Mary visits a fishing expert on the Isle of White before cooking lobster tails with asparagus. Plus, she makes a latte panna cotta that's perfect for special occasions. S1, Ep1
Mary spends time with two crayfish experts in Oxfordshire. Plus, she cooks a whole seabass with lime and makes refreshing elderflower jellies. S1, Ep3
Mary shares her favourite Easter recipes, including hot cross buns and roast lamb. Plus, she looks at how Christian communities celebrate Easter with food. S1, Ep1
Mary explores the wonderful foods that bring each different community together on Easter Sunday. Then, she uses this as inspiration to cook her own feast. S1, Ep2
Rick Stein travels to Naples. He cooks the beautifully light pasta con vongole. Plus, he ventures to Suffolk and cooks cod with bacon and cabbage. S1, Ep1
Rick Stein travels to Goa to explore the ways they cook fresh fish. He cooks turbot vallée d'Auge. Plus, he visits Cornwall and cooks a Goan lobster masala. S1, Ep2
Rick Stein travels to Galicia, Spain to explore their seafood dishes. He cooks Almejas a la Marinera. Plus, he visits Leigh-on-Sea and cooks a cockle soup. S1, Ep3
Rick Stein returns to Goa where he cooks a shark vindaloo and dhal with lentils. Plus, he travels to America and cooks Hangtown fry. S1, Ep4
With over-fishing now considered a major global crisis, chef Jamie Oliver is on a mission to convince home cooks and professionals to avoid common seafood and be more experimental.
Fish Stew And Asian Beef. On the menu are flashy fish stew, saffron sauce and garlic bread. Plus, seared Asian beef, best noodle salad and ginger dressing. S1, Ep2
Fish Baps And Mushroom Farfalle. Jamie makes his best fish baps, mushy peas and tartare sauce. Later he cooks mushroom farfalle with blue cheese and a hazelnut and apple salad. S1, Ep5
White Fish Tagine And Spicy Cajun Chicken. Jamie cooks white fish tagine, with carrot, mint and clementine salad. Plus, Spicy Cajun chicken, smashed sweet potato and corn salsa. S1, Ep31
Asian Fish And Ultimate Pork Tacos. Jamie shows how to make Asian fish, miso noodles and crunchy veg. Plus, the ultimate pork tacos, spicy black beans and avocado garden salad. S1, Ep38
